semanticCore: Regret, remorse, penitence, companionship, or sorrow
quranicRelevance: Becoming regretful for hasty/bad actions (Nadimeen - 'fa-asbaha mina an-nadimeen', 'fatusbihoo 'ala ma fa'altum nadimeen').

keyDerivations.meaning: To regret, feel remorse, repent, or be sorry
Regret, remorse, or sorrow of conscience

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ (5:31): So he became of the regretful (Cain after murdering Abel and realizing his inability to cover the corpse).
discardedNoise: Close companions and friends (Nadeem) in royal courts.
Modern psychological therapies for guilt complexes.
summaryEn: The root represents regret (Nadamah), capturing Cain's historical remorse, warning believers against hasty actions, and detailing the regret of the damned.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الندم وحسرة الضمير، مخلداً حسرة قابيل بعد قتل أخيه وعجزه عن مواراته (النادمين)، ومحذراً من التسرع بالفسوق.
rawText: مَنْدَمٌ Repentance: an inf. n. of نَدِمَ; see an ex. in a verse of El-Kattál El-Kilábee, cited voce أَىٌّ. نَدِمَ عَلَى مَا فَعَلَ 1 He grieved for what he had done; regretted it; repented of it: or he disliked it. (Msb.) نَدِيمٌ A companion in drinking; a cup-companion. (S, Msb, K.) And hence, Any convivial companion; a boon-companion.
